source_jobs.title: Contract Management & Revenue Integrity Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Overview

Nira Medical is a national partnership of physician-led, patient-centered independent practices committed to driving the future of neurological care. Nira's mission is to enable clinicians to provide access to life-changing treatments so you can provide the best possible patient outcomes. Founded by neurologists who understand the unique challenges of the field, Nira Medical supports practices with cutting-edge technology, clinical research opportunities, and a collaborative and comprehensive care network dedicated to advancing the standard of care. As we enter the next phase of growth, our focus is on scaling our teams, services, and elevating the customer experience!



This is where you come in…

The Contract Management & Revenue Integrity Specialist has a strategic and operational role at the heart of Nira Medical’s financial performance. The ideal candidate brings deep knowledge of payer contracting, fee schedule structures, and revenue integrity principles, and thrives in a fast-paced environment where precision, cross functional collaboration, and proactive problem solving are essential. As our Contract Management & Revenue Integrity Specialist, you will serve as the single point of accountability for all things payer contracting and pricing. You will lead new contract negotiations, maintain and update allowable tables, and ensure our rates—across professional services, drugs, and ancillary—are accurately reflected in our systems. You will work closely with Revenue Cycle, Operations, Finance, and clinical teams to identify and resolve payment discrepancies, support payer projects, and deliver timely reporting that keeps leadership informed. This role is critical to protecting Nira Medical’s revenue and ensuring every dollar we’ve earned is captured correctly.



Here’s what you’ll be doing…

 - New Contracting & Negotiations: Lead all new payer contract negotiations, managing the end-to-end process from initial outreach through execution, ensuring terms align with organizational goals and market benchmarks.

 - Pricing & Rate Management: Establish and maintain fee schedules and contracted rates for professional services, drug/pharmacy, and ancillary services. Own rate-setting decisions in collaboration with Finance and clinical leadership.

 - Allowable Table Management: Maintain, update, and audit all payer allowable tables within Athena and any other relevant systems. Coordinate quarterly or monthly allowable schedule pulls based on payer requirements and internal cadence needs.

 - Mismatch & Variance Resolution: Identify, track, and resolve payment variances resulting from a mismatched allowable between contracted rates and actual payer remittances. Partner with billing and RCM teams to correct discrepancies and prevent recurrence.

 - Payer Relations & Collaboration: Serve as the primary point of contact for payer inquiries and projects related to contract pricing. Facilitate timely resolution of payer-initiated issues and proactively surface risks to leadership.

 - Reporting & Analytics: Generate and distribute regular reporting on contract performance, allowable schedules, out-of-network activity, and no-go status. Ensure leadership has the visibility needed for strategic decision-making.

 - Out-of-Network & No-Go Analysis: Conduct out-of-network analyses to evaluate financial exposure and inform contracting strategy. Maintain and distribute no-go reporting to flag payers or services where participation is not financially viable.

 - System Management (Athena & Beyond): Manage all updates within Athena related to contracts, allowable updates, payer configurations, and rate changes. Ensure system accuracy is maintained continuously, and changes are tested and validated upon implementation.



Here’s what we’re looking for…

 - Associate’s degree in healthcare administration, business, finance, or a related field; or equivalent relevant experience in payer contracting or revenue integrity

 - Minimum 3–

source_jobs.title: Front Office Medical Receptionist- Round Rock, TX
source_jobs.description:
Front Office Medical Receptionist – Round Rock, TX

 

About Central Texas Neurology Consultants- Nira Medical:
CTNC is dedicated to delivering outstanding patient care in a collaborative, supportive environment. We pride ourselves on professionalism, efficiency, and compassionate service, making our clinic a trusted choice for patients in the Round Rock area.

 

Position Overview:
We are seeking a diligent and personable Front Office Medical Receptionist to join our team in Round Rock, TX. In this role, you will be the first point of contact for our patients, playing a key part in ensuring an exceptional experience from check-in to check-out. This position is ideal for individuals who are organized, detail-oriented, and committed to patient-centered service.

 

Key Responsibilities:

 - Greet patients warmly and provide outstanding customer service in person and over the phone

 - Manage patient check-in and check-out processes efficiently and accurately

 - Schedule appointments, verify insurance, and collect patient information

 - Assist with the completion and organization of patient forms and documents

 - Maintain confidentiality of all patient records and sensitive information

 - Support physicians, nurses, and other staff with administrative tasks

 - Ensure the reception area is clean, organized, and welcoming

 - Address patient questions or escalate needs to the appropriate staff member

 

Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or equivalent required

 - Prior experience in a medical office or similar setting preferred

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills

 - Strong organizational and multitasking abilities

 - Proficient with office software and electronic medical records (EMR) systems

 - Ability to work independently and as part of a team

 - Customer service oriented

 

Benefits:

 - Competitive compensation

 - Comprehensive benefits package

 - Supportive and team-oriented work environment

 - Opportunities for career growth and development

 - Paid time off

 

Location:
Round Rock, TX

 

CTNC values the contributions of every team member. If you are passionate about patient care and enjoy working in a fast-paced clinical setting, we encourage you to apply.

source_jobs.title: Billing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
The Billing Specialist (Biller) is responsible for the performance of a broad range of billing activities related to processing of claims for physician and practice-related ancillary services. The Billing Specialist will maintain expertise in claims submission, accounts receivable management, interpretation of related practice documentation, and other relevant tasks in order to support a best-in-class patient care platform. The Billing Specialist will also demonstrate proficiency in multiple applicable software systems in addition to growth in areas supporting physician services including physician-administered drugs, imaging, and other ancillaries.

Responsible To:

Director, Revenue Cycle Management

Education/Experience:

High School Diploma or equivalent (GED) required

Prior physician office and infusion drug experience highly preferred

Excellent interpersonal, communication and organizational skills required

Ability to prioritize, problem solve, and multitask is required

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Proactively submits and processes assigned third party payor billings (primary, secondary claims) to maximize accurate and timely billing

Achieves daily accomplishments that produce progress towards monthly, quarterly and annual cash collection and accounts receivable goals

Actively engages in assigned quality assurance tasks to safeguard accurate and timely claims creation including compliance with organizational policies and payor guidelines

Identifies unresolved or incomplete work product within assigned scope of responsibilities for follow up and escalation in a timely fashion

Identifies areas of opportunity or potential patterns of noncompliance during normal course of responsibilities and escalates appropriately for review

Anticipates most efficient resources and activities needed to secure payment of open claims and invoices including use of online payor policy research, electronic submission tools, or appropriate escalation or triage

 Performs other duties as required.

source_jobs.title: Medical Assistant
source_jobs.description:
MEDICAL ASSISTANT – NEUROLOGY CLINIC

Location: Round Rock, Texas
Schedule: Full time, Monday–Friday


POSITION SUMMARY

We are looking for a Medical Assistant to support our outpatient neurology practice. You’ll play a key role in patient care, clinic flow, and care coordination while working closely with neurologists and advanced practice providers. This is a fast-paced, hands-on role where MAs are trusted and valued members of the care team.

Key Responsibilities

Clinic & Patient Flow Support

 - Prepare patient charts ahead of scheduled visits by ensuring all relevant imaging, lab results, reports, and documentation are available and accurately loaded in the medical record.

 - Carry forward relevant clinical information into provider templates as appropriate to support efficient and high-quality visits.

 - Load imaging into the RadiAnt image viewer prior to patient visits so providers can seamlessly review studies with patients.

 - Room patients promptly and efficiently to support on-time clinic flow.

 - Perform patient intake, including review of medical history, completion of required quality/MIPS questions, medication reconciliation, and collection of vital signs.

 - Provide clear handoff to providers prior to the patient encounter.

 - Turn over exam rooms between patients, ensuring rooms are clean, stocked, and ready for the next visit.

Provider & Procedure Support

 - Prepare procedure trays as needed for in-office procedures and injections (e.g., Botox, Xeomin).

 - Assist providers during patient visits and procedures as requested.

 - Answer and manage clinical phone calls for assigned provider(s).

Clinical Administration & Care Coordination

 - Triage provider inboxes, including patient portal messages, administrative communications, and incoming forms, routing items appropriately to providers or other team members.

 - Obtain prior authorizations for oral and injectable medications using CoverMyMeds and other payer portals.

 - Coordinate specialty pharmacy medication delivery in advance of scheduled appointments (e.g., Botox, Xeomin, other injectable therapies).

 - Coordinate peer-to-peer reviews between providers and insurance companies when required.

 - Fax orders for imaging, laboratory tests, and other services as needed.

 - Pre-fill FMLA, disability, and other medical forms for provider review and signature.

 - Draft appeal letters and supporting documentation for insurance and benefits-related denials on behalf of providers.

Inventory & Medication Management

 - Receive medication orders and enter them into the inventory management system.

 - Manage medication samples, including proper storage, logging, and reordering in compliance with clinic policies.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Medical Assistant certification or diploma (CMA, RMA, CCMA or similar)

 - Experience in a medical office or specialty clinic

 - Strong organization, multitasking, and communication skills

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment and supporting complex patients

Neurology, injections, phlebotomy, or prior authorization experience is a plus, but we will train the right person.




WHY JOIN US

 - Monday–Friday schedule (no nights or weekends)

 - Competitive pay and benefits including 401k matching up to 8%

 - Supportive team and positive culture

 - Opportunity to grow as the practice expands

Apply today to grow your career in specialty care.

source_jobs.title: Neurologist (Neuromuscular / EMG / Neurophys) – No Call, 100% Outpatient – Austin, TX
source_jobs.description:
Neurologist – Neuromuscular / EMG / Neurophys (Outpatient, No Call) – Austin, TX

Step into an established neuromuscular program with immediate demand and procedural volume.

 - 4+ month waitlist for neuromuscular patients

 - Consistent EMG volume from day one

 - Established ALS clinic (MDA partnership)

 - Onsite infusion center supporting complex neurologic care

 - Active clinical research program

This is a neuromuscular-forward role within a fully outpatient model designed for neurologists who want to focus their practice.

Who this is for

This role is designed for a neurologist who wants to build or deepen a neuromuscular-focused practice in a high-demand environment.

Strong fits include:

 - Fellowship-trained neuromuscular neurologists

 - Neurologists with clinical neurophysiology / EMG experience

 - General neurologists who are comfortable managing neuromuscular conditions and interested in expanding that portion of their practice

 - Interest in participating in clinical research, even if not pursuing a fully academic or research-focused career

You’ll do well here if you want:

 - A neuromuscular-forward case mix with access to consistent EMG volume

 - To grow your experience in complex neuromuscular disease over time

 - A pure outpatient model without hospital obligations

 - A subspecialized team, so you’re not covering the full breadth of neurology alone

This is not a broad general neurology role, your practice will naturally skew toward neuromuscular based on existing demand

There is existing infrastructure and peer support to help you continue developing neuromuscular and EMG expertise if that aligns with your long-term focus.

The Practice

 - 100% outpatient neurology

 - No call, no inpatient responsibilities

 - Strong regional referral base with built-in demand

 - Established infrastructure across EMG, infusion, ALS, and research

 - Experienced clinical and administrative support

You’ll join a physician-led local team, backed by a national neurology platform that provides:

 - Operational scale and stability

 - Growth support

 - Preserved clinical autonomy

Compensation & Structure

 - Guaranteed base salary (above market, based on experience)

 - Performance incentives tied to productivity

 - Sign-on and relocation support

Full benefits include:

 - Health, dental, vision

 - 401k with 8% match

 - PTO and CME

Schedule

 - 4 or 5-day workweek

 - No weekends

 - No call

Location – Austin, TX

Practice in a high-demand, referral-rich market while living in one of the fastest-growing cities in the U.S.

 - No state income tax

 - Strong public and private school options

 - Year-round outdoor lifestyle

About Nira

Nira Medical is a physician-led neurology platform focused on building high-quality, outpatient practices with strong subspecialty support, including infusion and research.

Our Austin-area practice reflects that model, with an established neuromuscular program, consistent EMG volume, an ALS clinic in partnership with the Muscular Dystrophy Association, and onsite infusion and research infrastructure already in place. We partner with neurologists to build sustainable, patient-centered practices while preserving clinical autonomy.

source_jobs.title: Neurologist – Neuromuscular / EMG / Neurophys/ Industry Research (Outpatient, No Call) – Denver, CO
source_jobs.description:
Neuromuscular Neurologist

Fort Collins, Colorado (Greater Denver Area)

Position Summary

An established outpatient neurology practice in Fort Collins, Colorado is seeking a Board Certified or Board Eligible Neurologist to join a growing subspecialty neurology program.

This opportunity offers immediate access to a large established neuromuscular patient panel, strong referral demand, in-office EMG capabilities, infusion infrastructure, and clinical research participation within a highly collaborative outpatient practice environment.

The ideal candidate will have fellowship training in Neuromuscular Neurology or strong EMG/neurophysiology experience with a demonstrated clinical interest in neuromuscular disease. This role is intentionally structured around neuromuscular medicine and offers the opportunity to build a predominantly neuromuscular-focused outpatient practice in a high-demand market with limited local subspecialty competition.

Opportunity Highlights

 - Take over an established neuromuscular patient panel of approximately 850+ patients

 - Significant unmet regional demand with limited local neuromuscular subspecialty access

 - Outpatient-only practice

 - No hospital call responsibilities

 - In-office EMG/NCS capabilities

 - Onsite infusion center supporting complex neurologic disease management

 - Dedicated clinical and administrative support staff

 - Clinical research with established infrastructure

 - Flexible scheduling options, including potential 4-day work week

 - Physician-centered practice model emphasizing clinical autonomy and sustainable growth

Clinical Responsibilities

Responsibilities include evaluation, diagnosis, treatment, and longitudinal management of patients with neuromuscular disorders, including but not limited to:

 - Peripheral neuropathy

 - CIDP and immune-mediated neuropathies

 - Myasthenia gravis

 - Motor neuron disease / ALS

 - Myopathies

 - Radiculopathy

 - Entrapment neuropathies

 - General neuromuscular consultative care

The physician will perform and interpret EMG/NCS studies utilizing established in-office EMG infrastructure.

There is flexibility to incorporate selected general neurology interests based on physician background and preferences; however, the primary focus of the role will remain neuromuscular medicine.

Research Opportunities

Interested physicians will have access to:

 - Established clinical trial infrastructure

 - Dedicated research coordinators and support staff

 - Opportunities to participate as Principal Investigator (PI) or Sub-Investigator (Sub-I)

 - Collaboration with experienced research leadership and operational teams

Research opportunities may include neuromuscular and related neurologic clinical trials.

Practice Environment

The physician will join a collaborative outpatient neurology group supported by:

 - Dedicated Medical Assistant support

 - Administrative and scheduling support teams

 - Established referral pipelines

 - In-office EMG capabilities

 - Infusion services infrastructure

 - Operational leadership focused on physician efficiency and reduced administrative burden

The practice emphasizes high-quality subspecialty care, collaborative culture, and long-term sustainable physician practice development.

Qualifications Required Qualifications

 - MD or DO degree

 - Completion of accredited Neurology residency program

 - Board Certified or Board Eligible in Neurology

 - Eligible for medical licensure in the state of Colorado

 - Clinical interest in neuromuscular disorders

Preferred Qualifications

 - Fellowship training in Neuromuscular or Neurophysiology Neurology

 - Interest in clinical research participation

Compensation & Benefits

 - Competitive compensation package

 - Productivity and research income opportunities available

 - Comprehensive benefits package

 - CME allowance and paid time off

 - Malpractice coverage

 - Relocation assistance available

 - Retirement benefits

About Fort Collins, C

source_jobs.title: Pharmacist in Charge – Specialty & Infusion Pharmacy
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview:

Integrated Pharmacy Solutions (IPS) is a Texas-based specialty pharmacy delivering high-touch, clinically focused services across the specialty infusion, home infusion, and retail pharmacy space. Operating from the Dallas-Fort Worth, TX metro, we serve physician practices, infusion suites, and patients nationwide with the speed, expertise, and personalized support that complex specialty therapies demand. We specialize in specialty medications — including biologics and IVIG formulations — across neurology, rheumatology, and other specialty therapeutic areas.

Job Overview:

We are seeking a full-time Pharmacist to serve as the primary clinical and operational lead for our pharmacy. This role is responsible for day-to-day pharmacy operations, regulatory compliance, and clinical oversight, while working collaboratively with providers and staff in a specialty clinic setting. The ideal candidate is a highly organized, detail-oriented pharmacist with strong leadership skills and a background in specialty or infusion pharmacy.

Qualifications

 - Active Pharmacist license in Texas, in good standing.

 - Eligible and willing to serve as Pharmacist in Charge.

 - Willing to obtain additional state licenses.

 - PharmD preferred (BS Pharm considered).

 - Minimum 1 year of experience in a specialty or infusion pharmacy setting preferred

 - Strong working knowledge of pharmacy laws and regulatory requirements.

 - Excellent organizational, communication, and leadership skills.

 - Thrives in a collaborative, fast-paced environment with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

Knowledge & Skills

 - Proficient with pharmacy software systems (CareTend).

 - Working knowledge of specialty pharmacy accreditation process.

 - Competent in Microsoft programs (Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc.)

 - Stronge leadership Skills to engage teams in quality improvement initiatives

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Work Environment & Location:
This position will be onsite.

Schedule: M-F 9a-5p

Compensation & Benefits:
Competitive salary, performance-based incentives, comprehensive benefits package, and opportunities for professional growth and development.

source_jobs.title: Enrollment & Credentialing Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Overview

Nira Medical is a national partnership of physician-led, patient-centered independent practices committed to driving the future of neurological care. Nira's mission is to enable clinicians to provide access to life-changing treatments so you can provide the best possible patient outcomes. Founded by neurologists who understand the unique challenges of the field, Nira Medical supports practices with cutting-edge technology, clinical research opportunities, and a collaborative and comprehensive care network dedicated to advancing the standard of care. As we enter the next phase of growth, our focus is on scaling our teams, services, and elevating the customer experience!



This is where you come in…

The Enrollment & Credentialing Coordinator plays a critical role in ensuring Nira Medical’s providers, locations, and services are fully credentialed, contracted, and revenue-ready across all payers. This role manages provider enrollment, contract updates, and payer-related operational tasks needed to support new hires, new locations, acquisitions, and organizational expansion. The coordinator ensures timely provider enrollment, contract execution, and adherence to payer requirements while proactively resolving credentialing- or contracting-related issues that may impact revenue. This position requires a hands-on, process-driven mindset with the ability to problem-solve, build scalable workflows, and collaborate effectively with providers, payers, and internal teams. As part of a growing RCM structure, the role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and can help strengthen Nira’s credentialing foundation with structure, accuracy, and operational discipline. The coordinator partners cross functionally with RCM, Operations, Billing, Corporate Development, and external payer partners to ensure compliance and support the organization’s evolving revenue cycle needs.



Here’s what you’ll be doing…

· Provider Credentialing & Enrollment: This role ensures all providers are fully credentialed and enrolled in accordance with state, federal, and payer-specific regulations. Responsibilities include maintaining an accurate credentialing database, tracking expirations and renewals, and managing complete enrollment workflows with Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial payers. The coordinator oversees CAQH maintenance, NPI and PECOS updates, and payer portal applications while monitoring enrollment timelines and following up with payers to prevent delays. All supporting documentation is kept current, organized, and accessible to internal teams who rely on enrollment status for revenue readiness.

· Compliance & Regulatory Oversight: The Coordinator ensures compliance with all payer credentialing requirements and regulatory standards while maintaining clean, audit-ready credentialing files. This includes preparing reports for leadership, supporting internal and external audits, and keeping a centralized tracking system with real-time updates on application status. The role ensures that documentation, payer correspondence, and operational updates are consistently accurate, organized, and compliant.

· Provider & Payer Relationship Management: This role serves as a key liaison between providers, payers, and internal revenue cycle teams, facilitating timely issue resolution and clear communication. The coordinator provides education to providers regarding reimbursement structures, contract terms, and credentialing expectations, ensuring that both clinical and operational stakeholders understand the impact of payer requirements on revenue and compliance.

· Revenue Cycle & Operational Collaboration: The coordinator partners closely with RCM teams to ensure provider enrollment and credentialing processes do not disrupt cash flow or claim submission readiness. This includes identifying and resolving credentialing-related payment issues, supporting onboarding for new providers and practice locations, coordinating payer setup

source_jobs.title: Collections and Payments Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Position Summary:

The Collections and Payments Specialist is responsible for the performance of a broad range of collection activities related to obtaining payment for past due health insurance claims. The Collections and Payments Specialist will maintain expertise in organizational collection policies, dispute resolution, appeals management, and payment reconciliation in order to support a best-in-class patient care platform. The Collections and Payment Specialist will also demonstrate proficiency in multiple applicable software systems in addition to growth in areas supporting physician services including physician-administered drugs, imaging, and other ancillaries.

Responsible To:

Director, Revenue Cycle Management

Education/Experience:

High School Diploma or equivalent (GED) required

Prior physician office and infusion drug experience highly preferred

Excellent interpersonal, communication and organizational skills required

Ability to prioritize, problem solve, and multitask is required

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Proactively performs collections activities in accordance with established guidelines to interact with third party payors and patients for the purpose of collecting past due health insurance claims

Achieves daily accomplishments that produce progress towards monthly, quarterly and annual cash collection and accounts receivable goals

Actively engages in assigned quality assurance tasks to safeguard accurate and timely collections creation including compliance with organizational policies and payor guidelines

Identifies and researches validity of past due and/or disputed claims to expedite appropriate action in securing timely payment

Identifies areas of opportunity or potential patterns of noncompliance during normal course of responsibilities and escalates appropriately for review

Negotiates payment plans, partial payments and extensions of credit with appropriate reports for escalation to management review

Performs other duties as required.

source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager: Rocky Mountain Region
source_jobs.description:
Overview

Nira Medical is a national partnership of physician-led, patient-centered independent practices committed to driving the future of neurological care. Nira's mission is to enable clinicians to provide access to life-changing treatments.

Our team of board-certified neurologists, nurse practitioners, nurses, and researchers work collaboratively to deliver innovative, personalized care. Patients trust us for our expertise and consistent delivery of high-quality, comprehensive treatment in a welcoming and professional environment.



Provider Relations & Marketing Manager

The Provider Relations & Marketing Manager is responsible for executing marketing strategies to drive referrals and ensure that prospective patients are successfully converted into scheduled appointments. This role requires a proactive approach to physician outreach, community engagement, in-office referral processing, and digital marketing to grow the patient base. The Marketing Manager will work closely with providers, administrative staff, and external partners to support practices in Utah and Colorado.



Responsible To:
General Manager



Education/Experience:

 - High School Diploma or equivalent (GED) required, Bachelor degree or equivalent preferred

 - Strong interpersonal, communication, and organizational skills required

 - 3+ years of prior sales experience required

 - Prior physician office and neurology experience highly preferred

 - Proficiency in digital marketing, social media management, and CRM tools preferred

 - Ability to multitask, analyze data, and develop marketing strategies



MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develops and executes marketing initiatives to increase patient referrals and appointment conversion rates

 - Builds and maintains relationships with referring providers, including primary care and specialty physicians, through regular outreach and engagement efforts

 - Identifies new referral sources and opportunities for practice growth through data analysis and market research

 - Plans and coordinates community outreach events, physician networking opportunities, and educational seminars to promote the practice

 - Manages digital marketing efforts, including website updates, social media content, and patient reviews responses

 - Identifies and resolves barriers to scheduling, such as insurance issues, provider availability, and patient concerns, to improve referral conversion rates

 - Tracks and analyzes referral trends to identify opportunities for process improvement and enhance marketing and outreach strategies

 - Performs other duties as required

source_jobs.title: Movement Disorders Neurologist – Austin, TX | DBS, Botox & Research
source_jobs.description:
MOVEMENT DISORDERS NEUROLOGIST

Round Rock, Texas (Greater Austin)
Established Referral Base | DBS + Toxins | Clinical Research Infrastructure

The Austin market continues to see significant demand for subspecialty movement disorders care, with limited fellowship-trained capacity and growing need for Parkinson’s disease management, advanced therapeutics, and clinical research access.

We are seeking a Movement Disorders Neurologist — or a neurologist with a strong movement disorders focus — to join our established outpatient neurology and clinical research program in Round Rock, Texas, just outside of Austin.

This is a unique opportunity to expand upon an existing movement disorders practice alongside an established, nationally recognized movement disorders clinician and researcher with deep industry relationships, active clinical trials, and a substantial existing referral network.

You will step into immediate demand with an established waitlist, robust sponsor-supported research infrastructure, and the ability to practice advanced movement disorders medicine in a highly collaborative, physician-centered environment.


WHY THIS OPPORTUNITY STANDS OUT

 - Established and growing movement disorders referral base

 - Immediate access to a substantial patient waitlist

 - Work directly alongside a nationally recognized movement disorders researcher

 - Active industry-sponsored clinical trial program already in place

 - DBS programming with support from all major device representatives

 - In-office botulinum toxin program already operational

 - Outpatient-only practice -- no hospital or call duties

 - Monday–Friday schedule

 - Strong operational and research infrastructure with dedicated support staff

 - Opportunity to meaningfully shape and expand a movement disorders center in one of the fastest-growing regions in the country


CLINICAL FOCUS

This role is intentionally structured around movement disorders care, including:

 - Parkinson’s disease

 - Atypical parkinsonian

 - Essential tremor

 - Dystonia

 - Huntington's disease

 - Tourettes/Tics

 - Ataxias

 - Tardive dyskinesia

 - DBS programming

 - Botulinum toxin procedures

There is flexibility to incorporate selected general neurology interests based on your experience and preferences, but the core identity of this role is movement disorders medicine.


RESEARCH OPPORTUNITIES

This position is ideal for physicians who want to integrate meaningful clinical research into their practice without having to build infrastructure independently.

You will have access to:

 - Established sponsor relationships

 - Dedicated research coordinators and operational support

 - Existing and upcoming movement disorders and dementia trials

 - Built-in research patient flow from an active referral base and waitlist

Depending on experience and interest, the role may include:

 - Principal Investigator (PI) responsibilities

 - Sub-Investigator (Sub-I) participation with mentorship and development support

Research participation is encouraged, but the structure can be tailored to your background and long-term goals.


PRACTICE ENVIRONMENT & SUPPORT

You will join a collaborative outpatient neurology group that prioritizes physician support, sustainable growth, and subspecialty development.

Infrastructure includes:

 - Dedicated MA support

 - Established referral pipelines

 - Structured clinical ramp-up

 - NP support as your panel matures

 - In-office DBS programming support

 - In-office toxin program

 - Experienced research operations team

 - Administrative support for scheduling, referrals, and research coordination

This is a highly collegial environment designed for physicians who value collaboration over competition.


IDEAL CANDIDATE

 - MD or DO

 - Board Certified or Board Eligible in Neurology

 - Movement Disorders fellowship preferred, but not required

 - Strong clinical interest and experience in movement disorders

 - Interest in DBS and/or toxin management

source_jobs.title: Florida Multiple Sclerosis 100% Outpatient
source_jobs.description:
Overview

Since its founding, the MS & Neuromuscular Center of Excellence has been a leader in neurological care, earning a reputation for exceptional outcomes and compassionate service. Recognized for clinical excellence, we provide a seamless, patient-first experience that sets us apart.

Our team of board-certified neurologists, nurse practitioners, nurses, and researchers works collaboratively to deliver innovative, personalized care. Patients trust us for our expertise and consistent delivery of high-quality, comprehensive treatment in a welcoming and professional environment.

To ensure convenience and state-of-the-art care, we offer in-house infusion therapy and advanced diagnostic services, including NCVs and EMGs, with a focus on patient comfort. Additionally, we provide access to groundbreaking clinical trials for investigational treatments, offering hope and new possibilities for those with conditions such as Multiple Sclerosis and Myasthenia Gravis.

Specializing in Multiple Sclerosis, Neuromuscular, and General Neurology, our practice combines advanced technology, compassionate care, and a commitment to improving lives.

Why Join MS & Neuromuscular Center of Excellence?

Practice Autonomy with Support: Deliver high-quality, patient-centered care in an independent setting, with access to robust infrastructure and resources to enhance your practice.

Comprehensive Care Model: We emphasize a seamless patient journey, offering integrated services like infusion therapy and clinical trials all in one location.

Work-Life Balance: A manageable caseload, flexibility, and a strong team culture ensure you can focus on both professional fulfillment and personal well-being.

About the Role

We are seeking a fellowship-trained Multiple Sclerosis neurologist to join our growing network of dedicated neurologists. This role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a collaborative, physician-led environment committed to exceptional patient care.

Your Role Includes:

 - Performing neurologic history and physical exam and conducting neurodiagnostic tests as necessary to diagnose complex medical problems with a focus on multiple sclerosis 

 - Order and interpret neurologic tests including EEG, EMG, and neuroimaging studies (CT, MRI, and PET) 

 - Prescribe and/or administer medication and treatment for neurologic disorders such as multiple sclerosis including infusion therapy 

 - Monitor the side effects of treatment with knowledge of high efficacy disease-modifying therapy for multiple sclerosis 

 - Counsel patients and caregivers on neurologic diagnoses and disorders with a focus on multiple sclerosis 

 - Interact with primary care and other medical specialists to coordinate patient care 

 - Consult other specialists when necessary 

 - Monitor and track the patient’s response to treatment 

 - Carefully document the patient’s health care journey 

 - Respond appropriately to urgent and emergent situations as presented by phone and in person at the clinic 

 - Participate in lifelong learning  

 - Lead the neurologic care team which may include staff members and advanced practice providers 

  

Qualifications:

 - Completed a three-year residency in a US ACGME accredited neurology program 

 - Successful completion of the USMLE 

 - One- or two-year neurology fellowship in multiple sclerosis 

 - ABPN Board-certified neurologist (will consider Board-eligible if less than two years since residency/fellowship training completed). 

 - Clinical research experience as a PI or Sub-I is preferred but not required

 - Current medical license and eligible to obtain Florida medical license.  

 - Current federal DEA license  

 

What we offer  

We believe that great care for patients begins with happy providers. We strive to be the best place to work for neurologists.  

  

Exceptional salaries plus bonuses. In addition, we offer a competitive benefits package of health, dental, & vision insurance, annual CME allowan

source_jobs.title: Clinical Research Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Rocky Mountain Multiple Sclerosis Clinic is seeking an experienced Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC) to manage Neurology studies. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to EDC data entry, scheduling, chart preparation, conducting rating scales, collecting vital signs, blood draws, and ECGs.

Key duties include but not limited to:

 - Responsible for the integrity and overall quality of assigned clinical research trials

 - Serves as the primary contact for study subjects and sponsors, communicating daily as needed.

 - Reviews study protocols and brochures; creates study document binder and prepares source documents including study and subject information folders.

 - Attend sponsor meeting as needed for assigned protocols.

 - Oversee sponsor database process.

 - Completes study procedures including:

 - Pre-screening, screening, and study visits in conjunction with Physician/Investigator (may include vital signs, performing EKG’s, phlebotomy, processing specimens and shipping to labs).

 - Documentation of patient progress in response to investigative agents.

 - Coordination of monitor visits with patients.

 - Completion of source documents and case report forms.

 - Oversee preparation of physician orders to ensure protocol compliance as needed.

 - Maintain communication with physician regarding study requirements, dose modifications, and adverse events.

 - Upkeeps all trial regulator documentation.

 - Maintain high level of understanding of the ICH/GCP guidelines.

 - Perform other related duties as assigned

Qualifications

 - A minimum of 2 year's relevant work experience as a Clinical Research Coordinator.

 - This person must be highly detailed oriented, possess stellar organizational and prioritization skills, have excellent verbal and written communication and can work in a fast paced environment under limited supervision.

 - Skilled with Electronic Medical Record (EMR

 

Preferred:

 - At least 2 years of experience as a Clinical Research Coordinator or similar role; Neurology experience a strong plus. CCRC certified a strong plus

 - Prior working knowledge of research methodology, medical terminology and pharmacology.)

 

What We Offer

 - Full Time, 40 hour, Monday to Friday work week.

 - Competitive Salary with generous paid time off.

 - Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance

source_jobs.title: Regional Operations Leader (Healthcare) – Multi-Site Practice | Midwest | Michigan | Ohio
source_jobs.description:
REGIONAL OPERATIONS LEADER (HEALTHCARE) – MULTI-SITE NEUROLOGY PRACTICE

Lead operations for a ~$100M multi-site practice at a pivotal moment of transition and growth.

This is a senior operator role for someone who knows how to step into complexity, stabilize performance, and scale a market without losing culture.

You will partner directly with our Medical Director (dyad model) to lead all non-clinical operations across the region—owning performance, developing leaders, and driving both near-term execution and long-term growth.

This role is ideal for healthcare operators currently in roles such as:

 - General Manager

 - Director of Operations

 - Vice President of Operations

 - COO (single market or regional scope)


YOUR ROLE

You will operate as the senior operational leader for the market, with full responsibility for:

 - Clinic performance (revenue, EBITDA, patient experience, team metrics)

 - Leadership development across clinic managers and non-clinical teams

 - Execution of operational and growth initiatives

 - Acting as the primary bridge between the market and Nira’s central teams

You’ll need to be equally comfortable:

 - In the details (clinic flow, staffing, execution gaps)

 - At altitude (strategy, prioritization, scaling decisions)


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (FIRST 6–12 MONTHS)

 - Stabilize operations during a period of change while maintaining team engagement and morale

 - Establish strong, consistent performance across clinics

 - Build trust with physician partners and clinic leaders

 - Identify and execute on key levers to improve revenue and EBITDA

 - Successfully implement centralized initiatives locally (growth, infusion, research)

 - Strengthen leadership bench and team structure to support scale


WHAT YOU BRING

 - 10+ years of healthcare operations experience, with multi-site responsibility

 - Proven track record of leading teams through change or transition

 - Strong financial acumen, including P&L ownership and performance optimization

 - Experience working closely with physicians and APPs in a dyad or similar model

 - Ability to balance hands-on execution with strategic thinking

 - Comfort operating in a growing, evolving environment where not everything is fully built

Preferred:

 - Experience in neurology, oncology, infusion, or buy-and-bill environments

 - Experience in private equity-backed or high-growth healthcare settings


ABOUT NIRA

Nira Medical is a physician-led national neurology platform focused on building high-quality, outpatient practices with strong subspecialty support, including infusion and research.

Our model combines local physician leadership with centralized operational support, allowing markets to scale while maintaining clinical autonomy. This region represents a key part of that growth strategy, with an established foundation and significant opportunity ahead.

Compensation
Competitive compensation package aligned with performance, including base salary, bonus, and long-term growth incentives. Relocation support is available for candidates moving to the region.

source_jobs.title: PRN - Pharmacist
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW:

Central Texas Neurology Consultants is the premier provider of Neurological services in the greater Central Texas region. CTNC has been in practice for over 20 years and serves more than 10,000 people. Our on-site pharmacy and infusion center provides first-class, compassion focused care to patients receiving infusion therapy.




JOB OVERVIEW:

We are seeking a PRN Pharmacist to oversee operations at our in-office pharmacy. This role is ideal for a highly responsible and detail-oriented pharmacist who can provide leadership, ensure regulatory compliance, and support safe and efficient medication services on an as-needed basis.

This role will be the primary pharmacist responsible for the pharmacy’s day-to-day operations, compliance, and clinical oversight while collaborating with providers and staff in a clinical office setting.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active Pharmacist license in Texas, in good standing.

 - Eligible and willing to be designated as Pharmacist in Charge.

 - PharmD preferred (BS Pharm considered).

 - Minimum 1 years of pharmacy experience in healthcare-management role in specialty pharmacy or infusion pharmacy is preferred.

 - Strong knowledge of pharmacy laws and regulatory requirements.

 - Excellent organizational, communication, and leadership skills.

 - Ability to work independently in a satellite/solo pharmacist setting.


KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS

 - Proficient with pharmacy software systems (CareTend)

 - Working knowledge of specialty pharmacy accreditation process.

 - Competent in Microsoft programs (Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc.)

 - Stronge leadership Skills to engage teams in quality improvement initiatives

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Work Environment & Location:
This position will be on-site at the in-office pharmacy.

source_jobs.title: Outpatient Neurology Nurse Practitioner (NP) / Physician Assistant (PA) – Tampa, FL
source_jobs.description:
Overview

The MS & Neuromuscular Center of Excellence is a high-performing outpatient neurology practice built around subspecialty care in Multiple Sclerosis and Neuromuscular disease. Our model is designed to give advanced practice providers the ability to do meaningful, focused clinical work with the infrastructure and support to do it well.

You’ll work alongside a collaborative team of neurologists, APPs, and nurses in a setting that prioritizes continuity of care, strong patient relationships, and clinical quality. With in-house infusion services, EMG/NCV diagnostics, and access to clinical trials, you’re supported by a system that allows you to practice at a higher level without unnecessary barriers.

This is an environment where APPs are trusted, supported, and able to grow within a subspecialty-driven practice.

Job Description:

As an advanced practice provider, you will be an integral part of the care team and work in tight partnership with Medical Doctors. You will work with both general neurologists and specialized neurologists. You will be responsible for evaluating, diagnosing, and managing patients with a variety of neurological needs in the outpatient setting

 - Perform neurologic history and physical examinations and specialized diagnostic and/or therapeutic procedures, including botulinum toxin A injections, trigger point injections, and nerve blocks.

 - Prescribe and/or administer medication and treatment for neurologic disorders, including infusion therapy

 - Monitor the side effects of treatment

 - Counsel patients and caregivers on neurologic diagnoses and disorders

 - Interact with primary care and other medical specialists to coordinate patient care

 - Consult neurosurgery and other specialists when necessary

 - Monitor and track the patient’s response to treatment

 - Carefully document the patient’s health care journey

 - Respond appropriately to urgent and emergent situations as presented by phone and in person at the clinic

 - As applicable, participate in neuroscience research to better serve patients

Qualifications:

 1. Up-to-date Nurse Practitioner or Physician Assistant certification

 2. Current Florida state license as a Nurse Practioner or Physician Assistant or ability to get one

 3. Current federal DEA license

 5. Experience in neurology preferred, but not required. We provide great training.

What We Offer

We believe great patient care starts with clinicians who are supported, respected, and able to sustain their work long-term.

Lifestyle That Actually Holds Up
Outpatient-only practice with Monday–Friday daytime hours. No call, no weekends. A predictable schedule that allows you to fully disconnect outside of work.

Compensation & Benefits
Competitive compensation starting at $130,000+, with flexibility based on experience. Full benefits package including health, dental, and vision insurance, 401(k) with 8% matching, annual CME allowance, and a generous PTO and holiday schedule.

Daily Support That Makes a Difference
Free catered lunch provided daily and a team-based environment designed to reduce administrative burden and support your workflow.

Training & Growth
Structured onboarding with extensive training in neurology, including exposure to subspecialty care in MS and neuromuscular disease. Ongoing support to help you build confidence and expand your clinical scope.

Manageable, Sustainable Workload
We prioritize reasonable patient volumes and work closely with providers to ensure a caseload that supports both quality care and long-term sustainability.

source_jobs.title: Benefits and Authorizations Specialist Lead
source_jobs.description:
Job Summary: The Benefits Specialist Lead is responsible for verifying patient insurance coverage, submitting pre-authorizations, and financial assistance support  to ensure patients receive coverage for medical and infusion services provided.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Verify and document insurance eligibility, benefits, and coverage for all office visits and infusion services.

 - Obtains insurance authorization and pre-certification for office visits and infusion services.

 - Facilitate insurance denial mitigation steps such as peer-to-peer reviews and appeals.

 - Maintains a good working knowledge of infusion drug authorization requirements for all payers, state and federal regulatory guidelines for coverage and authorization.

 - Calculate and communicate patient financial responsibility.

 - Provide financial assistance support to patients including identifying patient assistance programs and manufacturer copay assistance enrollment.

Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or equivalent

 - 2-3 years of experience in medical insurance verification and prior authorizations.  Experience in infusion services is preferred.

 - ·       Knowledge of insurance terminology, plan types, structures, and approval types

 - ·       Previous experience with J-codes, CPT and ICD-10 coding

 - Previous Athena use a plus, but not a requirement

 - ·       Knowledge of medical terminology and clinical documentation review

 - Strong organizational skills

 - Detail-oriented

 - Ability to multi-task and work well in a fast-paced setting

 - Critical thinking skills and decisive judgment

source_jobs.title: Full Time Infusion Nurse (RN) – Outpatient Clinic – Salt Lake City, UT
source_jobs.description:
INFUSION NURSE (RN) – OUTPATIENT NEUROLOGY INFUSION CENTER

Salt Lake City, UT | Full Time | No Weekends | No Nights

If you’re an infusion nurse who’s burned out on hospital pace, rotating schedules, or high patient ratios, this role is intentionally different.

We are seeking an RN to join our outpatient neurology infusion center in Salt Lake where the focus is on thoughtful patient care, manageable patient volume, and a predictable weekday schedule. This is a structured, low-volume clinical environment designed to support both patients and nurses.

 


WHY NURSES ARE INTERESTED IN THIS ROLE

 - No weekends

 - Consistent 4-day schedule

 - Outpatient-only setting

 - Low patient volume with manageable workflow

 - Typically care for 2–3 patients at a time

 - Calm, team-oriented clinical environment

 - Opportunity to build meaningful patient relationships


CLINICAL ENVIRONMENT

This is not a high-throughput infusion center. The clinic is intentionally structured for safe staffing and sustainable workloads.

 - Approximately 7–9 infusion patients per day across the clinic

 - Specialty neurology infusion therapies

 - Infusion appointments ranging from approximately 1–5 hours


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Start IVs and administer specialty infusion therapies

 - Monitor patients during treatment and respond appropriately to infusion reactions

 - Manage care across 2–3 infusion chairs simultaneously

 - Provide patient education and support throughout treatment

 - Collaborate closely with providers, clinical staff, and infusion leadership

 - Maintain accurate clinical documentation and infusion safety protocols

 - Help support efficient, patient-centered infusion workflows as the program grows


TRAINING & SUPPORT

Even experienced infusion nurses receive structured onboarding and support.

Training includes:

 - 3 days of virtual infusion training

 - 7 days of in-clinic shadowing with experienced infusion staff

 - Gradual transition into independent patient care responsibilities

 - Clinical support from experienced infusion nurses and leadership


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active, unrestricted RN license

 - Strong IV skills required

 - Prior infusion, oncology, ED, ICU, or high-IV-volume experience preferred

 - Ability to work independently within a collaborative outpatient team

 - Strong patient communication and clinical judgment skills


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive compensation

 - Full benefits package including medical, dental, and vision

 - 401(k) with up to 4% employer match

 - Paid holidays and PTO

 - Predictable weekday schedule with no weekends or call

This opportunity is ideal for an RN seeking a more sustainable clinical environment while continuing to utilize and strengthen infusion and IV skills in a specialized outpatient setting.

source_jobs.title: Outpatient Neurology Multiple Sclerosis
source_jobs.description:
Overview 

Nira Medical is a national partnership of physician-led, patient-centered independent practices committed to driving the future of neurological care. Nira's mission is to enable clinicians to provide access to life-changing treatments so you can provide the best possible patient outcomes.  

Founded by neurologists who understand the unique challenges of the field, Nira Medical supports practices with cutting-edge technology, clinical research opportunities, and a collaborative and comprehensive care network dedicated to advancing the standard of care. 

Why Join Nira Medical Group? 

Practice Autonomy with Support: Physicians deliver high-quality, patient-centered care in an independent setting, with access to robust infrastructure and resources to enhance their practice. 

Comprehensive Care Model: Nira emphasizes a seamless patient journey, offering integrated services like infusion therapy, physical therapy, and clinical trials all in one location. 

Innovative Brain Health Leadership: Our model of care is driven by a vision to lead the nation in brain health, supporting practice expansion, ancillary services, and research. 

Work-Life Balance: A manageable caseload, flexibility, and a strong team culture ensure you can focus on both professional fulfillment and personal well-being. 

About the Role 

We are currently seeking fellowship-trained Multiple Sclerosis and Alzheimer’s Specialists to join our growing network of dedicated neurologists. While this is our current focus, we are open to connecting with all neurologists who are aligned with our mission. This role offers the opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a collaborative, physician-led environment committed to exceptional patient care. 

 

Your Role Includes: 

 

 - Performing neurologic history and physical exam and conducting neurodiagnostic tests as necessary to diagnose complex medical problems with a focus on multiple sclerosis  

 - Prescribe and/or administer medication and treatment for neurologic disorders 

 -  Monitor the side effects of treatment with knowledge of high efficacy disease modifying therapy 

 - Counsel patients and caregivers on neurologic diagnoses and disorders with a focus on multiple sclerosis  

 - Interact with primary care and other medical specialists to coordinate patient care  

 - Respond appropriately to urgent and emergent situations as presented by phone and in person at the clinic  

 - Participate in neuroscience research to better serve patients  

 - Lead the neurologic care team which may include staff members and advanced practice providers  

   

Qualifications: 

 - Completed a three-year residency in a US ACGME accredited neurology program  

 - Successful completion of the USMLE  

 - One- or two-year neurology fellowship in applicable subspecialty 

 - ABPN Board-certified neurologist (will consider Board-eligible if less than two years since residency/fellowship training completed).  

 - Current medical license and eligible to obtain new state medical license.   

 - Current federal DEA license   

 - Professional organization membership is preferred  

  

Skills  

 - Outstanding listening skills  

 - Compassionate counseling skills  

 - Detail oriented documentation  

 - Superb organizational and time management skills  

 - Able to supervise other clinicians and team members  

  

What we offer   

We believe that great care for patients begins with happy providers. We strive to be the best place to work for neurologists.   

   

Exceptional salaries plus bonuses. In addition, we offer a competitive benefits package of health, dental, & vision insurance, annual CME allowance, 401K, and generous vacation & holiday package.   

   

We know the value of work-life balance and work closely with providers to ensure a reasonable caseload.   

Location 

Our clinics are located in some of the country’s most desirable cities, offering a high quality of life, outdoor recreation, and vibrant c

source_jobs.title: Part-Time Infusion Nurse (RN) – Outpatient Clinic – Austin, TX
source_jobs.description:
Part Time Infusion Nurse (RN) – Outpatient Neurology
Location: Austin, TX

If you enjoy infusion nursing but want a calmer environment, meaningful patient interaction, and a schedule that actually works for you, this role is built for that.

Central Texas Neurology Consultants is hiring a Part-Time Infusion Nurse to support our growing outpatient infusion center. This is a flexible, weekday-based role in a highly specialized neurology practice where quality of care—not volume—drives the day.

Why This Role Stands Out

• Low, manageable patient volume – No high-throughput infusion pace; time to focus on patient care
• Flexible Part Time scheduling – 2 10-hour weekday shifts per week
• No nights or weekends – Outpatient setting with predictable hours
• Specialized neurology care – Infusions for MS and other neurologic conditions (non-oncology)
• Strong, supportive team – Work alongside experienced neurologists and infusion nurses
• Meaningful patient relationships – Longer visits allow for real connection and continuity

What You’ll Do

• Administer specialty infusion therapies and perform patient assessments
• Start IVs and manage infusions with strong clinical judgment
• Monitor for infusion reactions and respond appropriately
• Educate patients and caregivers throughout treatment
• Collaborate with providers and the infusion team to ensure a seamless experience
• Maintain compliance with HIPAA and all clinical protocols

What We’re Looking For

• Active, unrestricted RN license
• BLS certification required
• 1+ year of nursing experience (infusion experience preferred)
• Strong IV skills and clinical judgment
• Comfortable working independently in a small team environment

What We Offer

• Competitive compensation
• Monday–Friday scheduling (no weekends or holidays)
• Flexible, predictable shifts
• A genuinely positive, team-oriented work environment
• Opportunity to grow with a well-established, expanding neurology practice

source_jobs.title: Neurologist (Neuromuscular / EMG / Neurophys) – No Call, 100% Outpatient – Tampa/Clearwater, FL
source_jobs.description:
Neurologist – Neuromuscular / EMG / Neurophys (Outpatient) – Tampa/Clearwater, FL

Step into an established neuromuscular panel with immediate volume in a referral-driven market.

 - ~60+ neuromuscular referrals/month with no active marketing

 - Direct patient inheritance from a departing neuromuscular neurologist

 - Established EMG volume and procedural demand

 - Robust infusion infrastructure (including IVIG)

 - Expanding clinical research program

This is a neuromuscular-forward role with the ability to maintain a balanced practice where appropriate—without being pulled into inpatient or call responsibilities.

Who this is for

This role is designed for a neurologist who wants to build or deepen a neuromuscular-focused practice within a high-demand, referral-driven environment.

Strong fits include:

 - Fellowship-trained neuromuscular neurologists

 - Neurologists with clinical neurophysiology / EMG experience

 - General neurologists who are comfortable managing neuromuscular conditions and interested in expanding that portion of their practice

 - Open to participating in clinical research, even if not pursuing a fully academic career

You’ll do well here if you want:

 - A neuromuscular-forward case mix with strong EMG volume

 - Flexibility to maintain a complementary mix of headache (including Botox), movement disorders, or general neurology within your comfort zone

 - A pure outpatient model without hospital obligations

 - A subspecialized team, so you’re not covering the full breadth of neurology alone

This is not a broad general neurology role—your practice will naturally skew toward neuromuscular based on existing demand

The Practice

 - 100% outpatient neurology

 - No call, no inpatient responsibilities

 - Collaborative group including neuromuscular, MS, epilepsy, and general neurologists

 - Strong regional referral base built over decades

 - Established infrastructure across EMG, infusion, and research

You’ll inherit a panel that includes:

 - 32% neuromuscular

 - Headache (including Botox)

 - Movement disorders

 - Dementia and general neurology

There is flexibility to shape your panel over time toward your areas of interest and expertise.

Procedures, Infusion & Care Model

 - EMG: Established in-house volume with consistent demand

 - Infusion: Strong infrastructure, particularly for neuromuscular indications (e.g., IVIG), supported by an experienced specialty pharmacy

This model supports:

 - Appropriate use of advanced therapies

 - Strong patient outcomes

 - A sustainable compensation structure without high visit volume pressure

Compensation & Structure

 - Competitive above-market salary

 - Incentives tied to:
   
   - Clinical volume
   
   - Research participation and program development

 - Sign-on and relocation support

 - Residency/fellowship stipend

 - Student-loan repayment

 - Full benefits package:
   
   - Health, dental, vision
   
   - Retirement plan w/ 8% match
   
   - PTO and CME

Compensation is transparent and flexible, with the ability to tailor structure based on your clinical focus and goals.

Schedule

 - Typically 5 days per week, with flexibility to move to 4 as the practice evolves

 - No weekends

 - No call

Location – Tampa / Clearwater / Lutz, FL

Practice in a high-growth, referral-rich market with strong demand for subspecialty neurology.

 - Desirable coastal lifestyle with access to beaches and outdoor recreation

 - Rapid population growth driving continued patient demand

 - Limited local access to neuromuscular and movement disorder specialists, positioning you as a key regional resource

About Nira

Nira Medical is a physician-led neurology platform focused on building high-quality, outpatient practices with strong subspecialty support, including infusion and research.

Our Tampa-area practice reflects that model, with an established referral network, robust infusion capabilities, growing research infrastructure, and a collaborative subspecialty t

source_jobs.title: 100% Outpatient Neurology Texas - Vibrant Community 20 Miles North of Austin!
source_jobs.description:
Since its founding in 2004, Central Texas Neurology Consultants (CTNC) has been providing innovative, advanced care for patients and families with a wide range of brain, spine, nerve and muscle disease. We aim to promote health and education as we support our patients, caregivers, family members, healthcare providers and community. We want the best for our patients, which is why we conduct clinical research to extend the knowledge and treatment of neurological disorders. 

 

To provide patients with the highest level of care, every care plan is tailored at their unique needs and preferences. Our collaborative team structure includes award-winning board-certified neurologists, nurse practitioners, nurses, researchers, and physical therapists.    

 

In 2024, CTNC partnered with Nira Medical Group, a national partnership of physician-led and patient-centered independent practices focused on driving the future of neurological care delivery. Empowered by technology, Nira’s mission is to provide access to life changing treatments for those suffering from MS, Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, and similar disorders so that they can lead healthier, more fulfilling lives.  

 

Located in Round Rock, Texas, CTNC enjoys the benefits of a vibrant community just 20 miles north of Austin. This prime location offers a unique blend of suburban charm and urban convenience, making it an ideal place to live and work. Round Rock features excellent schools, diverse recreational activities, and a welcoming atmosphere, while Austin's cultural richness, including music, art, and dining, is easily accessible. You'll enjoy an enriching lifestyle and be part of a collaborative healthcare team dedicated to making a difference in patients' lives    

 

  

Job Description:  

  

As an MS-specialist, you will be responsible for evaluating, diagnosing, and managing MS-patients along with a group of general neurology patients.  

 

 - Performing neurologic history and physical exam and conducting neurodiagnostic tests as necessary to diagnose complex medical problems with a focus on multiple sclerosis 

 - Order and interpret neurologic tests including EEG, EMG, and neuroimaging studies (CT, MRI, and PET) 

 - Prescribe and/or administer medication and treatment for neurologic disorders such as multiple sclerosis including infusion therapy 

 - Monitor the side effects of treatment with knowledge of high efficacy disease modifying therapy for multiple sclerosis 

 - Counsel patients and caregivers on neurologic diagnoses and disorders with a focus on multiple sclerosis 

 - Interact with primary care and other medical specialists to coordinate patient care 

 - Consult other specialists when necessary 

 - Monitor and track the patient’s response to treatment 

 - Carefully document the patient’s health care journey 

 - Respond appropriately to urgent and emergent situations as presented by phone and in person at the clinic 

 - Participate in neuroscience research to better serve patients 

 - Participate in lifelong learning  

 - Lead the neurologic care team which may include staff members and advanced practice providers 

 

  

Qualifications:  

 - Completed a three-year residency in a US ACGME accredited neurology program 

 - Successful completion of the USMLE 

 - One- or two-year neurology fellowship in multiple sclerosis 

 - ABPN Board-certified neurologist (will consider Board-eligible if less than two years since residency/fellowship training completed). 

 - Current medical license and eligible to obtain Utah medical license.  

 - Current federal DEA license  

 - Professional organization membership is preferred 

 

 

What we offer  

We believe that great care for patients begins with happy providers. We strive to be the best place to work for neurologists.  



We offer generous salary and bonuses and includes a competitive benefits package of health, dental, & vision insurance, annual CME allowance, 401K, and generous vacation & holiday

notes: Nira Medical is a national partnership of physician-led, patient-centered independent practices committed to driving the future of neurological care.
